name: Signeasy eSignature API
what_it_does: Mobile-first electronic signature platform with REST API — enabling businesses to send documents for e-signature, manage templates, track signing workflows, and embed signing in applications, with OAuth2 authentication, webhooks for status updates, and strong mobile app experience for iOS/Android signature capture.
use_cases: Sending documents for e-signature via Signeasy API for business contract automation
Embedding signing workflows via Signeasy API for in-app document signing experiences
Managing signing templates via Signeasy API for standardized agreement deployment
Tracking document signing status via Signeasy API for contract lifecycle visibility
Automating HR document workflows via Signeasy API for onboarding and compliance
not_for: Enterprise CLM with complex approval chains (use DocuSign or Adobe Acrobat Sign for CLM)
High-volume document automation at scale (pricing may be limiting)
Jurisdictions requiring specific eIDAS QSCD signatures
best_when: A mobile-forward SMB or startup needs an affordable, easy-to-use e-signature API with strong mobile experience — particularly teams in India or APAC where Signeasy has strong market presence.
avoid_when: You need enterprise CLM, high-volume bulk signing, or advanced identity verification.

auth.notes: OAuth2 authorization code flow. Client ID + Client Secret for app registration. Access token via /oauth/token. Bearer token in Authorization header. Scopes: rs.read, rs.write (for documents). Refresh token support. Webhook events for document signed, declined, viewed. Developer portal at developers.signeasy.com. Sandbox environment available for testing.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free trial: 5 free documents; no ongoing free tier
pricing.paid_tiers: Business $8/user/month (billed annually); Business Plus $15/user/month; API plans: contact sales
pricing.notes: Indian company (Bengaluru). Founded 2010 (Sunil Patro). 48,000+ businesses. 1M+ users. Strong India and APAC presence. US market growing. ESIGN/UETA compliant. HIPAA compliant (Enterprise). SOC2 Type II. Mobile-first: iPhone app was top-rated for field signature collection. Box, Dropbox, Google Drive, OneDrive integrations. G Suite Marketplace. Microsoft Teams integration. Zapier integration. API access for Business Plus and above. Raised $8M funding. Relatively affordable vs DocuSign for SMB.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: OAuth2 with scopes. Sandbox environment. Webhooks. HIPAA on Enterprise. SOC2. Indian company. Mobile-first. API on Business Plus+ plan. ESIGN/UETA. Sandbox for testing.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Document reads idempotent by document ID. Signing status reads idempotent. Document sending not idempotent. Webhook events: implement idempotent handlers.
known_agent_gotchas: API access requires Business Plus plan ($15/user/month) or higher
Sandbox environment available — use separate API credentials for testing
OAuth2 scopes: rs.read for reading, rs.write for sending documents
Indian company — GDPR compliance for EU data; US/EU/IN data residency options
Mobile-first strength: strong for field signature collection use cases

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. OAuth2 with scopes. ESIGN/UETA legal compliance. HIPAA on Enterprise. SOC2 Type II. Indian company with US/EU/IN data residency. Signed documents are legally binding records. Mobile-first design — signature captured on mobile devices. Sandbox for safe testing without production data.
